title: Second Day, Steady Hands

mood.label: Uneasy calm 🌥️
mood.summary: A second day of open US-Iran fighting kept the ledger heavy, but active mediation and a market that shrugged and climbed lifted the mood a single, honest notch.

style.genre: brass-led dark electronic / cinematic drum-and-bass — a sampled muted trumpet and low brass tracing a circling E harmonic minor topline over a broken, dub-spaced RolandTR909 pulse with deep tape-echo, a sampled vibraphone pricking mallet accents in the gaps, a plucked upright bass walking under a soft sine sub, and a low bowed-string pad holding the dread; harmonic minor's raised seventh (D#) dragging hard against its lowered sixth (C) — the exact half-step tension of a day that trades fire for a second day yet lets mediators and a rising market pull it back a notch, neither breaking nor resolving

sampleSources: Strudel default library — VCSL brass & mallets, RolandTR909, sampled piano
pattern: stack( s("bd ~ ~ bd ~ ~ bd ~, ~ ~ sd ~ ~ ~ sd ~, hh*8").bank("RolandTR909").gain(perlin.range(0.4,0.62).slow(9)).room(0.24).delay(0.28).delaytime(0.19), note("<[e2,g2,b2,d3] [c3,e3,g3] [a2,c3,e3] [b2,ds3,fs3]>").s("gm_trumpet").attack(0.06).sustain(0.55).release(0.5).lpf(sine.range(700,2100).slow(14)).gain(0.28).room(0.4).rsize(3), note("e5 ~ g5 fs5 ~ e5 ~ b4 c5 ~ b4 ~ ds5 ~ e5 ~").s("gm_trumpet").lpf(2400).gain(0.3).attack(0.02).release(0.35).delay(0.32).delaytime(0.1875).off(0.125, x=>x.gain(0.14).s("vibraphone").lpf(3200)), note("e2 ~ e2 b2 ~ g2 ~ a2 e2 ~ b2 ~ c3 ~ b2 ~").s("sawtooth").lpf(560).gain(0.34).attack(0.01).release(0.2), note("e1 ~ ~ ~ e1 ~ ~ ~").s("sine").gain(0.4).lpf(200), note("<[e3,b3] [c4,g4] [a3,e4] [ds4,b4]>").s("sawtooth").attack(1.4).sustain(0.8).release(2.5).lpf(sine.range(360,900).slow(18)).gain(0.13).room(0.5).hpf(140) )

lines: Second morning, same low sky, the sirens didn't stop,
ninety targets in the dark, a bridge, a port, a drop.
Ten missiles chase a base at dawn, the count climbs, small and grim,
and still two quiet phone lines hum, asking them back in.
Steady hands on a shaking line, a notch, not a fall,
the market shrugs and climbs a stair while the sirens call.
Hold the ledger open, let the half-step ring —
one point up on an uneasy day, and that's the whole thing.
Gaza's ceasefire keeps its dead, El Obeid keeps its dark,
Venezuela lights the candles, Bavi bends the park.
But a pill you swallow beats the needle, Patriots get made,
so the number lands at forty-one, buoyed and afraid.
